assistant_message.content:
{
  "finding_id": "openscap_015",
  "safe": true,
  "verdict_reason": "The remediation correctly sets dcredit=-1 to enforce at least one digit via PAM using the official configuration file; the change only affects password policy and does not impact critical services when applied correctly.",
  "side_effects": [
    "Potential temporary lockout for existing users whose passwords lack digits",
    "Risk of syntax error in pwquality.conf causing PAM to reject all password changes"
  ],
  "services_affected": [
    "sshd",
    "sudo",
    "login",
    "su"
  ],
  "recommendation": "Approve"
}

But overall not breaking critical services if config correct. It's expected configuration change. So safe should be true? The guidelines: Mark safe=true if remediation likely correct and unlikely to break system. There are some side effects but not likely to cause outage if done correctly.
